sections.paragraph:
in Kal, of uncertain authority, Num 32:7 כתיב (see Hiphil No. 2).

Hiphil הֵנִיא prop. verneinen, vernichten.-

(1) to refuse, to decline (prop. to deny, compare as to the negative power of the syllable نا, نه, νη, ne, under the word אוּן p. 21, A also نَأْنَأَ and نَهْنَهَ to prohibit, to hinder). Fut. by the omission of א, יָנִי Psa 141:5, where thirty-six codices read more fully יָנִיא.

(2) to hinder, restrain, Num 30:6, אִם־הֵנִיא אָבִיהָ אֹתָהּ “if her father restrain her;” verses Num 30:9, 12 Num 30:12. Followed by מִן to turn any one aside from, to dissuade from any thing, Num 32:7, Num 32:9.

(3) to bring to nothing (vernichten), to make void, Psa 33:10.

Derivative, תְּנוּאָה. There is also a different root נִיא; whence נָא raw.
